examples.title: Proper SIGCHLD Handler
examples.description: SIGCHLD handler that handles multiple child terminations
examples.explanation: This handler properly handles multiple child terminations by using waitpid with WNOHANG in a loop. The SA_RESTART flag restarts interrupted system calls, and SA_NOCLDSTOP prevents delivery when children stop.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of terminated children

examples.title: Fork Multiple Children
examples.description: Parent forks multiple children and handles their termination
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates proper handling of multiple child processes. The parent forks 5 children, and the signal handler uses waitpid with WNOHANG to reap any terminated children, preventing zombies.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of child processes

examples.title: Double-Fork Technique
examples.description: Using double-fork to prevent zombies and create daemon-like process
examples.explanation: The double-fork technique creates an orphan process adopted by init. The intermediate child exits immediately without waiting for the grandchild, preventing zombie creation. This is useful for launching daemons.

examples.title: SA_NOCLDWAIT Example
examples.description: Using SA_NOCLDWAIT to automatically reap children
examples.explanation: This demonstrates SA_NOCLDWAIT flag which causes terminated children to be automatically reaped without becoming zombies. The trade-off is that exit status cannot be collected.
